message:
fix(web): add missing OG metadata so X/Twitter renders card images

OG share images worked on iMessage and Telegram but not on X.
Investigation revealed three issues:

1. Missing required OpenGraph protocol fields — og:type, og:url, and
   og:site_name were never emitted because the root layout had no
   openGraph metadata block. X's crawler is stricter about OG protocol
   compliance than iMessage/Telegram crawlers.

2. Duplicate title suffix — child layouts included " | Agent Flywheel"
   in their title strings, but the root layout's title template already
   appends " | Agent Flywheel", producing
   "… | Agent Flywheel | Agent Flywheel" in og:title and twitter:title.

3. Stale X card cache — X caches card images aggressively and the
   convention-file content hash only changes when image code changes.
   Added lib/og-version.ts which derives a per-deploy cache-buster
   from VERCEL_GIT_COMMIT_SHA (falls back to Date.now() locally).
   Layouts now set explicit openGraph.images and twitter.images with
   ?v=<version> so every Vercel deploy produces fresh image URLs that
   force X to re-crawl.

Root layout changes:
- Added openGraph: { type: "website", siteName, locale, url }
- Added twitter: { card: "summary_large_image", creator: "@doodlestein" }

complete-guide/layout.tsx:
- Removed redundant " | Agent Flywheel" from title
- Added openGraph.type: "article", openGraph.url
- Added explicit openGraph.images and twitter.images with cache buster

tldr/layout.tsx:
- Removed redundant " | Agent Flywheel" from title

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,12 @@
+/**
+ * Deploy-time cache buster for OG/Twitter image URLs.
+ *
+ * X (Twitter) caches card images aggressively. The convention-file content hash
+ * only changes when the image code changes. This version string changes on
+ * every Vercel deploy, forcing X to re-crawl fresh images.
+ *
+ * Usage in layout metadata:
+ *   openGraph: { images: [{ url: `/page/opengraph-image?v=${ogVersion}`, ... }] }
+ */
+export const ogVersion =
+  process.env.VERCEL_GIT_COMMIT_SHA?.slice(0, 8) ?? String(Date.now());
